considered_alternatives.reason: Independent review operationalization would build on strategic registries that still contained coherence drift.
Objective coverage hardening had lower immediate governance value than repairing authoritative strategic metadata.

rationale: Repair authoritative strategic registry coherence before extending independent review governance or opening lower-priority objective coverage work.
roadmap_debt: Objective coverage remains partial.
Strategic evidence quality remains weak.
Capability intelligence remains the dominant roadmap track.

considered_alternatives.reason: Deferred pending 66C completion; designated corrective path for objective coverage gaps per SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005.

rationale: Calibrate strategic review finding rules: SRS-RULE-007 recalibrated to fire only on active growth (not completed-track depth); SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005 suggested_resolution updated to name 64H as designated corrective path. Close BR-004 (independent_review_governance) at advisory level. Scope constrained per IRG challenge review: no workflow coupling, review remains advisory, no broader operationalization.
roadmap_debt: Broader independent review workflow operationalization not included (deferred per IRG challenge review constraints).
BR-004 advisory closure represented in governance_boundaries; registry status remains active until 66C completes.
Recommended next phase: 64H — Multi-Runtime Objective Coverage Hardening.

rationale: Strategic Review findings SRS-RULE-004 and SRS-RULE-005 from 66C (per SRR-66B-001 authority) identified OBJ-003 primary_count=1 as a classification artifact from the 65D bulk mapping. 64H was the designated corrective path per SRS-RULE-004/005 suggested_resolution text. Cross-track movement from BR-004 closure (66C) to BR-001 resumption (64H) requires strategic lineage per the 65J model. IRG challenge review approved_with_changes; scope constrained to reclassification proposals + evidence reports; direct_map_mutation_allowed=False.
roadmap_debt: SRS-RULE-004 (partial completeness) not cleared by 64H; future primary-adding phases required.
SRS-RULE-006 (evidence quality) may improve only when evidence reports are consumed by strategic review model.
Reclassification proposals OCH-PROP-64H-001 through OCH-PROP-64H-005 require human approval before map update.

rationale: Recent phases (64H series) repeatedly surfaced post-‘implementation complete’ defects (stale lineage fixtures, task contract scope violations, evidence inconsistency, review supersession ambiguity), demonstrating that IRG review state should be automatically visible in the governed workflow rather than human-recall-dependent. The original automated-review-loop proposal was challenged twice by IRG self-review and reduced to minimum viable scope: surface the latest non-superseded review state in session bootstrap only, with review_blocks_any_operation=False. This extends BR-004 into a second arc (review loop visibility) per explicit human decision; no new branch created.
roadmap_debt: pcae check / task transition / phase handoff IRG surfacing deferred to a future phase decision.
Phase activation gate, lineage write gate, and roadmap mutation gate explicitly deferred.
Warning-fatigue mitigation (display-state suppression) deferred until multi-trigger surfacing exists.

rationale: Automatic visibility without workflow authority was explicitly approved for IRG Challenge. 66E extends BR-004 from bootstrap-only review visibility into deterministic, advisory-only challenge surfacing at summary-oriented lifecycle boundaries so assumptions, blind spots, inconsistencies, counterfactuals, and uncertainty are displayed without changing approvals, readiness, or command outcomes.
roadmap_debt: pcae check integration remains deferred to avoid hidden workflow authority.
No challenge persistence, lineage, override, acknowledgement, or remediation workflow is introduced in 66E.
Attention-fatigue refinement beyond bounded summary output remains future work.

rationale: 66E deterministic challenge engine was identified as generating the same questions every session regardless of governance evolution, undermining skepticism quality. 67A upgrades to comparative assessment (new/persistent/resolved), historical confidence model (high/medium/low epistemic reliability), contradiction synthesis via synthesis anchors and tension patterns, persistent-concern suppression after threshold phases, and differentiated surface strategy per lifecycle boundary. derived_history_is_historically_observed=False; confidence_modifies_attention_level=False.
roadmap_debt: Challenge persistence remains stateless; no archived challenge history introduced.
Resolved concern derivation is not implemented (stateless engine cannot derive resolutions).
Attention-level escalation with age explicitly rejected (regression_finding_implies_failure=False).

rationale: 67A identified that IRGC measures challenge generation but not challenge impact — it can classify new/persistent/resolved concerns but cannot detect whether challenge output appears correlated with governance evolution. 67B adds a non-binding correlation observation layer: domain-aligned artifact detection, temporal proximity signals, CorrelationPatterns (public), and calibration effects. InfluenceHypothesis is computed internally and never surfaced; humans see correlation, not influence. The principle: the engine may reason about influence internally, but output must remain correlation-framed. No persistence, no blocking, no governance authority.
roadmap_debt: Calibration effects (suppression_weight_delta) are computed but not applied — actual suppression modification deferred to a future phase.
Resolved concern derivation remains stateless (inherited from 67A).
Temporal proximity uses phase sort-key gap, not calendar time — coarse but deterministic.

rationale: 67B introduced a challenge impact assessment layer (correlation observations, calibration effects) but the IRGC engine still allocates attention uniformly across all findings regardless of informational value. 68A designs a deterministic, stateless attention allocation layer: AttentionSignals classify informational characteristics (novelty, contradiction, correlation strength, domain diversity); AttentionBudgets constrain per-surface output; AttentionDecisions distinguish surface from background without implying governance significance. visibility_is_importance=False; suppression_is_resolution=False; attention_allocation_affects_governance=False; no persistence, no blocking, no governance authority.
roadmap_debt: AttentionDecisions are computed but not yet applied to render_irg_challenge_compact_lines output — actual surface filtering deferred to a future phase.
Calibration effects from 67B remain unapplied; their interaction with attention allocation deferred.
Persistent attention memory explicitly rejected; future phases must re-evaluate if compelling evidence emerges.

rationale: 68A designed the adaptive attention allocation layer but left AttentionDecisions unconnected to the rendering path. 68B wires the allocator into automatic compact rendering surfaces (bootstrap, handoff, completion) via render_irg_challenge_compact_lines_with_allocation(). Explicit pcae irg-challenge command and JSON output retain all findings. allocator_changes_visibility_only=True; findings_unchanged_by_allocator=True; suppressed_findings_remain_available=True; attention_allocation_does_not_affect_command_outcomes=True. No governance outcomes are changed.

branch_closure.closure_observation: BR-004 reached a level of sophistication that exceeds current finding volume. Future challenge-system enhancements should be justified by observed contention rather than architectural possibility.
branch_closure.descoped_debt: 67B calibration effects (suppression_weight_delta) are computed but not applied. Explicitly descoped from BR-004 closure criteria. Future work requires separate branch justification.
branch_closure.objectives_achieved: Independent review governance operational. Challenge generation, comparison, impact analysis, attention allocation, effectiveness review, and competitive persistence rebalancing completed across phases 66A-68D. OBJ-001 and OBJ-002 served throughout: review_output_is_binding=False, execution_allowed=False.

rationale: 68C effectiveness review identified that persistent_background signal was computed but not actively used as a demotion criterion in _irca_allocate_attention() Step 3. Persistent findings competed equally with non-persistent findings during diversity_fill, reducing repetition reduction to incidental rather than structural. 68D adds competitive persistence demotion: Step 3 sort key becomes (is_persistent_background, domain, finding_id) so non-persistent findings precede persistent ones when budget is binding. Demotion is conditional — persistent findings surface unchanged when no non-persistent alternatives compete. demotion_is_scheduling_not_priority_ranking=True. persistence_implies_lower_priority=False maintained. Completion surface bypass documented as intentional via _IRCA_COMPLETION_SURFACE_NOTE. Rejected: hard demotion (makes absolute governance claim), weight-based scoring (implies importance ordering), rotational visibility (requires persistence). This closes the structural gap identified in 68C and completes the BR-004 attention arc.

BR-004 retrospective identified that PCAE now governs itself extremely well but the largest remaining capability gap is governed execution of real runtime activity. The Controlled Runtime Execution Pilot (62A) is dormant; multi-runtime governance infrastructure (BR-001) is complete but no actual execution flows through it. 69A is a design-only architecture review: what prevents PCAE from governing actual execution? The question is about governance readiness, not execution implementation. BR-005 Execution Governance Activation opens on this finding.

69A identified that gep-gate-001 (prompt_approved) was permanently blocked because no ApprovedPromptArtifact runtime store existed. 69B implements the MVT approval store: persistent JSON storage under .pcae/approvals/, _apa_validate enforcing approval_state, approved_by, approved_at, and snapshot completeness, and CLI surface pcae approval-store write. gep-gate-001 and gep-gate-005 are wired to the live store; authorization_status advances to conditionally_authorized when both human-approval gates satisfy. execution_allowed=False invariant preserved throughout.
roadmap_debt: 69B clears approval-storage blockers only. Execution authorization remains incomplete: invocation contract validation (gep-gate-007) and approved-agent validation (gep-gate-006 partial) are unresolved. These are deferred to a future phase (target: 69C).
Execution activation remains out of scope; 69B is an authorization-foundation phase, not an execution phase.
Approval index, supersession mutation, and approval history queries deferred.
Full validation_snapshot and governance_snapshot population deferred (placeholder used).
Provenance event integration deferred.

rationale: 69C advances BR-005 by validating the remaining non-executing invocation prerequisites left open by 69B. Scope is intentionally exclusive: approved-agent validation (gep-gate-006), invocation-contract availability (gep-gate-007), codex-local contract verification, claude-local contract verification, and runtime contract registry consistency. Execution activation remains out of scope and execution_allowed=False remains invariant.
roadmap_debt: 69C validates invocation prerequisites only; execution activation remains out of scope.
execution_allowed=False remains mandatory throughout 69C.
No runtime invocation, prompt execution, or write authorization is introduced by 69C.

rationale: Integrates all four required gates (001/005/006/007) into a single governed pathway via build_execution_pathway_integration(). Authorization conditioned on all four gates; execution_allowed=False in all outputs.
Records authorization artifacts before execution for EAR-based authorization verification. execution_allowed=False maintained throughout.
Records execution audit trail (EAR) for each governed execution attempt. execution_allowed=False maintained throughout.

First controlled execution path in PCAE history: claude-local, read-only, single-agent, 11-condition gate, contract-authoritative command from _ICONV_VALIDATED_CONTRACTS, ERR store at .pcae/results/. execution_allowed=True inside invoke_readonly_execution boundary only; EAR remains immutable; ERR-based re-execution prevention.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69G-001: no sandbox (SLR-69G-001)
SLR-69G-002: no rollback (SLR-69G-002)
SLR-69G-004: EAR-ERR cross-link deferred to 69H

rationale: Classifies ExecutionResultRecord outcomes on technical_status (success/failed/timeout/invocation_error/indeterminate) and governance_attention (clear/requires_attention/requires_review) axes. Ephemeral — no review persistence. SLR-69H-001: ExecutionResultReviewArtifact persistence deferred to 69I.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69H-001: ExecutionResultReviewArtifact persistence deferred to 69I

rationale: Introduces append-only ExecutionResultReviewArtifact (ERRA) store at .pcae/result-reviews/. Persists human reviewer disposition and ERG classification snapshot. Completes APA→ARA→EAR→ERR→ERG→ERRA governance chain. SLR-69I-001: multi-reviewer conflict resolution intentionally deferred. Conflicting reviews for the same ERR may coexist. Future governance phases may introduce conflict-resolution workflows. 69I preserves all review artifacts and performs no automatic reconciliation.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69I-001: multi-reviewer conflict resolution deferred to future phase

rationale: Introduces ExecutionSnapshotArtifact (ESA) store at .pcae/execution-snapshots/ and ExecutionChangeRecord (ECR) store at .pcae/execution-changes/. ESA captures pre-execution git working-tree state. ECR compares pre/post and classifies change_severity (none/low/medium/high/critical). rollback_candidate=True when severity in {medium,high,critical}. Manual command model (Option 1): no automatic integration with invoke_readonly_execution. SLR-69J-001: automatic snapshot integration deferred to future phase (69K candidate). rollback_executed=False; automatic_rollback_allowed=False; execution_allowed=False.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69J-001: automatic snapshot integration into invoke_readonly_execution deferred to future phase

rationale: Closes SLR-69J-001 by integrating ESA creation into invoke_readonly_execution before subprocess and ECR creation immediately after subprocess. ERR carries snapshot_id, snapshot_created, ecr_id, ecr_created linkage fields. git unavailability and timeout are advisory. snapshot_storage_failure blocks as condition_12_snapshot_storage_failed. ECR failure is advisory. execution_allowed semantics unchanged. _EASI_GOVERNANCE_BOUNDARIES enforces all boundaries. Manual snapshot/change commands remain fully supported. SLR-69K-001: snapshot storage failure blocks execution; no degraded-mode fallback exists. SLR-69K-002: post-execution capture may include changes from concurrent processes when sandbox_mode=none.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69K-001: snapshot storage failure blocks execution; no degraded-mode fallback exists
SLR-69K-002: post-execution capture may include changes from concurrent processes when sandbox_mode=none

SLR-69K-003: OS-level write exceptions in storage functions (store_execution_snapshot, store_execution_change_record, store_execution_result) are unhandled; filesystem write failures escape as unhandled exceptions rather than governed blocked returns with stored=False
SLR-69K-004: ECR stores execution_result_id before ERR is written; if ERR storage subsequently fails the ECR contains a dangling forward reference with no cleanup or tombstone mechanism

SLR-69K-005: change_record_id and snapshot_id generation use second-precision timestamps duplicated across automatic and manual paths; same-second invocations for the same prompt_id produce identical id field values; lookup functions return most-recent match and cannot distinguish automatic from manual artifacts

rationale: Introduces workspace isolation for invoke_readonly_execution via git worktree + rsync overlay. Subprocess runs with cwd=sandbox_dir not root. Condition 13 blocks on sandbox creation or rsync overlay failure. ERR schema expanded with sandbox_mode=workspace_isolation, sandbox_id, sandbox_provider. ECR post_state sourced from sandbox before destruction. Six SLR entries document forward-compatibility constraints for 69M write governance. SLR-69L-001: sandbox_provider fixed to git_worktree; container providers deferred. SLR-69L-002: os_isolation deferred; workspace_isolation is development containment only. SLR-69L-003: sandbox_dir ephemeral; forensic artifact copy deferred. SLR-69L-004: ECR captures paths not content; ECP class required in 69M for promotion. SLR-69L-005: workspace isolation is behavioral not OS containment; absolute path writes uncontained. SLR-69L-006: git worktree shares object store with root; git commits in sandbox persist in shared store.
roadmap_debt: SLR-69L-001: sandbox_provider fixed to git_worktree; container providers deferred
SLR-69L-002: os_isolation deferred; workspace_isolation is development containment only
SLR-69L-003: sandbox_dir ephemeral; forensic artifact copy deferred
SLR-69L-004: ECR captures paths not content; ECP artifact class required in 69M for promotion payload
SLR-69L-005: workspace isolation is behavioral not OS containment; absolute path writes and symlink write-through uncontained
SLR-69L-006: git worktree shares object store with root; git commits inside sandbox persist in shared store

rationale: Closes SLR-69L-004 by introducing ExecutionChangePackage (ECP), the canonical evidence artifact for sandbox-produced content (file content, diffs, before/after hashes, binary and symlink classification, per-file promotion_eligible decisions), and ExecutionPromotionReview (EPR), a human content-level review of a specific ECP with partial-path approval. Accepted scope is ECP + EPR only -- no promotion execution, no rollback execution, no git commit, no git push, no automatic promotion. Condition 14 is the first post-execution governance condition: ECP capture attempt is mandatory and ordering-enforced before sandbox destruction, but capture success is not mandated -- failure is always recorded (capture_outcome="failed") and surfaced as execution_reviewable=False rather than retroactively setting execution_occurred=False. Ten SLR entries document the accepted scope and forward-compatibility constraints for a future promotion-execution phase. SLR-69M-001: ECP is the canonical evidence artifact, captured from sandbox_dir before destruction, immutable after creation, does not modify root. SLR-69M-002: EPR records human review of ECP content with partial-path approval; promotion_authorized is a separate explicit flag with no consumer until promotion execution exists. SLR-69M-003: .git/ and .pcae/ are permanently excluded from ECP promotion eligibility; cannot be overridden by EPR. SLR-69M-004: external symlink promotion is permanently forbidden. SLR-69M-005: root divergence detection is required before any future promotion; divergence with conflicting files is a hard block. SLR-69M-006: git commits made inside the sandbox are detected via git_head_diverged and must block promotion. SLR-69M-007: promotion execution is deferred to a future phase (69N). SLR-69M-008: automatic_promotion_allowed=False is a permanent governance boundary, not specific to this phase. SLR-69M-009: before_content is captured per file entry as a rollback payload; rollback execution itself is deferred. SLR-69M-010: ECP capture attempt is mandatory and ordering-enforced (Condition 14) but capture success is not mandated; sandbox destruction proceeds regardless of ECP outcome, preserving the ESB-C-003/004 cleanup guarantees from 69L.

SLR-69M-002: EPR records human review of ECP content with partial-path approval; promotion_authorized is a separate explicit flag with no consumer until promotion execution exists

rationale: Implements Write Promotion Execution: PromotionExecutionRecord (PER) is the first PCAE artifact and `pcae promote` is the first command that mutates root. Promotion is gated on an EPR with promotion_authorized=True, never on an ECP alone. Divergence is checked per file by content hash rather than git HEAD alone: a path whose current root hash equals the ECP after_hash is already_applied and is skipped without being re-written or treated as an error -- this is how re-running `pcae promote` resumes a partial promotion, with no --resume flag and no additional authority expansion beyond what was approved. A path matching neither before_hash nor after_hash is a conflict that blocks the entire attempt before any file is touched. Partial promotion is a valid terminal state for mid-run write failures after divergence passed clean. PER is created with status=in_progress before the first file write and persisted after every file, so an interrupted run is always a stored, inspectable record, never silent. `pcae promotion-execution mark-interrupted` is bookkeeping only. Ten SLR entries document the accepted scope. SLR-69N-001: PER is the first artifact whose subject is root mutation; promotion gated on EPR promotion_authorized=True, not ECP alone. SLR-69N-002: divergence checked per-file by content hash; git HEAD divergence alone is advisory, not blocking. SLR-69N-003: already_applied (current hash matches after_hash) is skipped, not an error, not re-written -- resume-aware with no --resume command. SLR-69N-004: any per-file conflict blocks the entire attempt before any file is touched. SLR-69N-005: partial promotion is a valid terminal state, not automatically retried. SLR-69N-006: PER created before first write, persisted after every file; interruption is always a stored record. SLR-69N-007: mark-interrupted is bookkeeping only, never writes files. SLR-69N-008: EPR override_divergence is not consumed in 69N; conflicts are unconditionally blocking. SLR-69N-009: before_content/rollback_payload_available is evidence only; rollback execution is deferred. SLR-69N-010: no automatic promotion, no git commit/push, no multi-EPR batch promotion, no atomic staged-rename writer -- sequential write plus incremental PER chosen as the smaller mechanism.
